pages.title: About | Keir Mather MP
pages.meta_description: About page for Keir Mather MP, Member of Parliament for Selby and Kippax
pages.text_excerpt: --> About | Keir Mather MP top of page Keir Mather MP Phone Mail Phone keir.mather.mp@parliament.uk ​​ 01757 247 990 ​ 16 Park Street, Selby, YO8 4PW Home About Selby and Kippax North Yorkshire Council Leeds City Council Seeking support Local Campaigns SEND provision Road safety Improving mobile signal Tackling flooding Better buses Progress in Parliament Key Policy Statements News Contact Privacy Policy Standards About Keir Keir was elected as your Labour Member of Parliament for Selby and Kippax in the 2024 General Election. ​ Born and educated locally in Yorkshire, Keir moved on to to study History and Politics at Wadham College, Oxford before receiving a Master of Public Policy degree at University College, Oxford. ​ Keir was first elected in the Selby and Ainsty by-election in July 2023 and was returned as the Member of Parliament for the new Selby and Kippax constituency in the 2024 General Election, with a majority of 10,195. ​ ​ ​In Parliament, he has been fighting for the people of Selby on issues such as the cost of living crisis, SEND provision, rural crime, public transport and underperforming NHS services. Keir made his Maiden Speech in the House of Commons on 16th October 2023, during a debate on Early Years Provision . ​ Following the election of the Labour Government in July 2024, was appointed to HM’s Government as an Assistant Government Whip. In September 2025, Keir was appointed to serve as the Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State in the Department for Transport. ​ Keir lives in Selby and is a passionate supporter of the Rugby League team Hull KR. Stay updated on my work in the constituency and beyond Sign up for my newsletter © 2025 by Keir Mather MP. All rights reserved. Promoted by Keir Mather MP of 16 Park Street, Selby, YO8 4PW bottom of page

I'm campaigning for better connectivity and safer streets, I'm securing investment in flood defences for our villages and pushing for more support in schools. ​ Scroll down for the latest updates on these campaigns, and if you have something that you want me to campaign on in the future, then please do get in touch with me. h2: Pension Justice for British Coal Workers
pages.text_excerpt: --> Progress in Parliament | Keir Mather MP top of page Keir Mather MP Phone Mail Phone keir.mather.mp@parliament.uk ​​ 01757 247 990 ​ 16 Park Street, Selby, YO8 4PW Home About Selby and Kippax North Yorkshire Council Leeds City Council Seeking support Local Campaigns SEND provision Road safety Improving mobile signal Tackling flooding Better buses Progress in Parliament Key Policy Statements News Contact Privacy Policy Standards Progress in Parliament Proud advocate for Selby and Kippax in Parliament Keir Mather MP - Statement on SEND Reforms STATEMENT ON SEND REFORMS - Wednesday 25th February 2026 Each family has a different story to tell - but everyone who has navigated the SEND system in recent years knows that it is broken. This week, the government published its proposals to reform this broken system. I wanted to say something about the reforms because SEND has been a top priority for me as MP and I know how much it matters to many families in Selby and Kippax. I’m aware that for lots of local families the id keirmathermp Keir Mather MP welcomes Government’s new strategy to tackle rising road deaths This week, the Government published Britain's first Road Safety Strategy in over a decade, setting out the most far-reaching reforms to make our roads safer in a generation. The package will include advanced safety technologies for new cars, mandatory eyesight test for drivers over 70, a tightening of laws on drink driving, road safety education for local primary schools, a review of the MOT system, and updated guidance on local speed limits. As part of the publication, the G keirmathermp Pension Justice for British Coal Workers 621 people in the Selby and Kippax constituency will receive an uplift in their pensions thanks to a successful campaign led by local MP, Keir Mather. Yesterday, Chancellor Rachel Reeves announced in the 2025 Budget that the government will pay out the surplus in the pension pot of former British Coal staff. Last year’s budget saw an uplift in pensions for a large proportion of mineworkers. This year, the Government goes further to end pension injustice for members of the Bri keirmathermp Stay updated on my work in the constituency and beyond Sign up for my newsletter © 2025 by Keir Mather MP. pages.text_excerpt: --> News | Keir Mather MP top of page Keir Mather MP Phone Mail Phone keir.mather.mp@parliament.uk ​​ 01757 247 990 ​ 16 Park Street, Selby, YO8 4PW Home About Selby and Kippax North Yorkshire Council Leeds City Council Seeking support Local Campaigns SEND provision Road safety Improving mobile signal Tackling flooding Better buses Progress in Parliament Key Policy Statements News Contact Privacy Policy Standards Latest News March 2026 Newsletter Mar 31 Selby Times - March 2026 Like everyone else in the Selby area, I’ve been incredibly concerned by the unfolding conflict in Iran this past month. My thoughts have been with all of those impacted, including those British service personnel called into action for defensive measures, civilians facing airstrikes and the horrors of war, and British citizens in the region. I thought I would use my column this month to reflect on the position the British Government has taken, and to reaffirm my support for th Mar 26 Keir Mather MP - Statement on SEND Reforms STATEMENT ON SEND REFORMS - Wednesday 25th February 2026 Each family has a different story to tell - but everyone who has navigated the SEND system in recent years knows that it is broken. This week, the government published its proposals to reform this broken system. I wanted to say something about the reforms because SEND has been a top priority for me as MP and I know how much it matters to many families in Selby and Kippax. I’m aware that for lots of local families the id Feb 25 February 2026 Newsletter Feb 24 SELBY MP BREAKS GROUND ON TRANSFORMATIONAL LOCAL SEND SCHOOL Selby MP Keir Mather has broken ground on the new Selby SEND School located in Osgodby, North Yorkshire. South View Academy will offer specialist teaching for 100 children, aged 3-19 with special education needs (SEND). The school will be run by the Wellspring Academy Trust, which currently runs 33 schools across Yorkshire and Lincolnshire. Funding for the school was initially promised in 2019 but, under the previous Conservative Government, faced years of delays. Since being Feb 16 January 2026 Newsletter Jan 30 Local MP welcomes fresh start at York Hospital Selby MP Keir Mather visited York Hospital last week, and met with Clare Smith, the new Chief Executive of York and Scarborough Teaching Hospitals NHS Trust who joined the organisation in November. It follows positive news about falling NHS waiting lists, which now stand at their lowest level since 2023. Mr Mather, many of whose Selby constituents rely on York Hospital, visited the site both to hear from the new Chief Executive about her plans for the Hospital, and to represe Jan 20 Selby Times - January 2026 This is my first column of this year, so I’d like to take the chance to wish everyone a happy new year. December feels like a lifetime ago, but it’s not too late to give a special shoutout to Gethin from Athelstan Primary in Sherburn, who won my Christmas card competition and whose design appeared on the front of my card. It was a real joy to host Gethin and his family down in Westminster, where I gave them a tour of Parliament and introduced them to the Speaker of the House Jan 15 Local MP delivers on free breakfast club promise to make sure no child goes hungry. This week, the Department for Education has confirmed that two Selby schools will be included in the next phase of the Labour government’s free breakfast club programme. The two primary schools - Barwic Parade Community Primary School and Selby Community Primary School - were supported by their local MP to secure the government funded breakfast provision. Keir Mather MP wrote to the Education Secretary in December to submit his support for the two applications. pages.text_excerpt: --> Contact | Keir Mather MP top of page Keir Mather MP Phone Mail Phone keir.mather.mp@parliament.uk ​​ 01757 247 990 ​ 16 Park Street, Selby, YO8 4PW Home About Selby and Kippax North Yorkshire Council Leeds City Council Seeking support Local Campaigns SEND provision Road safety Improving mobile signal Tackling flooding Better buses Progress in Parliament Key Policy Statements News Contact Privacy Policy Standards Contact If you're a resident of the Selby and Kippax constituency and are in need of support, have any concerns about our local area or thoughts on policy issues, please do get in touch with me. ​ ​ Email: ​ keir.mather.mp@parliament.uk ​ Telephone: ​ 01757 247 990 ​ Constituency Address: The Office of Keir Mather MP 16 Park Street, Selby, YO8 4PW ​ Westminster Address: ​ Keir Mather MP, House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA ​​ In all correspondence, please ensure you provide your full name, residential address and any contact number; strict parliamentary protocol means I can only reply to constituents of Selby and Kippax. ​ Mail Home Location Camera Phone Stay updated on my work in the constituency and beyond Sign up for my newsletter © 2025 by Keir Mather MP.
